2025-04-04.thesis: China export controls on heavy rare earths, April 4
2025-04-04.exit_case: none — export controls just imposed April 4, curbs in force, no reversal
2025-04-04.assessment: Entry week. China imposed export controls on seven medium/heavy rare earths (samarium, gadolinium, terbium, etc.) via Announcement 18 on April 4, amid Liberation Day tariff war. MP Materials named as the lone scaled US producer — clean primary vehicle. This is a structural chokepoint on Western heavy-REE supply; the curbs create urgency for ex-China production. Keep full basket: MP (US scaled producer), LYSDY (Lynas, ex-China heavy REE), REEMF (Rare Element Resources), TMC (seabed nodules), UUUU (Energy Fuels REE separation). Early, under-the-radar amid broad tariff noise.
2025-04-04.exit_advice: Hold. China's Announcement 18 heavy rare-earth export controls just landed today — a specific, datable supply shock with clean US/ex-China pure-plays. Standing curb regime, driver freshly live.

2025-04-11.thesis: China export controls on heavy rare earths, April 4
2025-04-11.exit_case: None — heavy-REE export curbs remain fully in force; tariff pause exempts China, so US-China escalation actually deepened, not reversed.
2025-04-11.assessment: Week 2. No direct fresh coverage of the rare-earth trend this week — news is dominated by the broad tariff-pause rally. But the driving condition is undamaged: China is the sole country excluded from the tariff walkback, so the heavy-REE export controls remain in force and geopolitically hotter. Applying mild silence decay for lack of dedicated REE press this week, but the structural ex-China supply thesis is intact. Keep the full basket: MP (scaled US producer, cleanest pure-play), LYSDY (Lynas ex-China heavy REE), REEMF, TMC, UUUU (separation capability).
2025-04-11.exit_advice: Hold. The chokepoint is intact and freshly reinforced — Trump paused tariffs for everyone EXCEPT China, meaning the trade war (and thus China's leverage via rare-earth curbs) is escalating, not de-escalating. No reversal of the export controls. Structural driver still open-ended.

2025-04-11.thesis: CMS finalized better-than-expected 2026 Medicare Advantage rate hike
2025-04-11.exit_case: CMS final rate notice landed April 7 and insurers already rallied one day — the discrete catalyst has occurred and priced.
2025-04-11.assessment: Entered this week on CMS's finalized, better-than-expected 2026 Medicare Advantage rate hike (announced April 7). Major MA insurers rallied Tuesday on the news; CVS separately raised full-year guidance and named a new CFO, reinforcing the tailwind. The finalization is a discrete event that has occurred and drew immediate coverage, so much of the pop is priced — but the driver (higher 2026 reimbursement) feeds forward into upcoming bid filings and 2026 margin recovery, keeping the thesis live for now. All five names are legitimate MA-exposed vehicles; HUM and UNH are the most MA-levered pure-plays, with CVS/ELV/CI as diversified peers. Watch for the herd being fully arrived — if coverage saturates with no further concrete milestone, fade conviction.
2025-04-11.exit_advice: Hold — entry week; the better-than-expected 2026 rate benefit still flows forward into June MA bids and 2026 margins, not yet in realized earnings.

2025-04-18.thesis: China export controls on heavy rare earths, April 4
2025-04-18.exit_case: none — China's April 4 heavy-REE export controls remain in force; China is the sole nation excluded from the tariff walkback, no reversal
2025-04-18.assessment: Week 3. Another week with zero dedicated rare-earth coverage — news is entirely tariff-macro, telecom/defensive rotation, and Boeing/China aircraft retaliation. The driving condition is undamaged and arguably hotter: China stopped taking Boeing deliveries (2025-04-15), signaling escalating US-China decoupling, and China remains excluded from the tariff pause, so heavy-REE controls stand. But the structural thesis earns high conviction only by delivering fresh milestones, and there is none this week — applying silence decay, stepping conviction down again from prior. Keep the full same-catalyst basket: MP (cleanest scaled US pure-play), LYSDY (Lynas ex-China heavy REE), REEMF, TMC, UUUU (separation capability). No vehicle's own thesis has broken.
2025-04-18.exit_advice: Hold. The export-curb regime is a structural chokepoint still fully in force; no counter-event (curbs lifted / China carve-out) has occurred. Exit only on a genuine reversal, not on quiet weeks.
